channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: 5 Common TypeScript Mistakes in React (And How to Fix Them)
item.description: 5 Common TypeScript Mistakes in React (And How to Fix Them) TypeScript has become the de facto standard for React development, and for good reason: it catches bugs at compile time, improves IDE...

item.title: I built a LinkedIn network analyzer in 6 days using science from 1973. Here's what I found.
item.description: Most job seekers apply to hundreds of jobs and hear nothing back. The research explains why. A 2022 experiment on 20 million LinkedIn users proved that the people most likely to get you a job...

item.title: March 24, 2026: SMS became a native language.
item.description: With SML, we describe UI declaratively. With SMS, we wire behavior and events. Today, after performance testing, we decided to create an SMS compiler. SMS -> LLVM IR -> native code local trusted...

item.title: 80+ Free Coding Challenges Built Around Real-World Problems (Not Just Algorithms)
item.description: Most coding challenge platforms are algorithm-heavy. That's not a bad thing — but after working on real projects, the problems that actually slow you down tend to look different. Debugging code...

item.title: My latest project is Live! Checkout FileCrank.app
item.description: FileCrank is a free, browser-based file processing toolkit built for people who need to get things done without installing software or creating accounts. Open a tool, upload your file, and...

item.title: I built a system that stops AI actions before they execute
item.description: Most AI systems today don’t just generate text. They actually take actions. They send emails, trigger workflows, move data, and even change infrastructure. And almost all of them follow the same...

item.title: Vibe Coding Is Powerful, but Technical Skills Still Matter
item.description: Vibe Coding Is Powerful, but Technical Skills Still Matter AI-assisted development is changing how we build software. Today, it's possible to describe an idea in plain language and get working UI,...

item.title: Double-Texting Anxiety: When Your Attachment Style Hijacks Your Phone
item.description: The Second Text You Wish You Hadn't Sent You sent a text. They haven't responded. It's been 47 minutes. You check if it was delivered. It was. You check if they're online. They are. Your body...

item.title: Building Taskmaster: A Go-Powered Process Supervisor from Scratch
item.description: How two 42 School students reimagined process management with Go's concurrency model If you've ever managed a production server, you know the pain: a critical process crashes at 3 AM, no one...

item.title: Introducing L-BOM and GUI-BOM
item.description: The AI software landscape and the broader development communities are in a serious period of change. While one could argue that open-source development has steadily evolved over the last two...

item.title: Arm’s first CPU ever will plug into Meta’s AI datacenters later this year
item.description: After decades of only licensing its chip designs for others to use, UK-based Arm revealed the first chip it's producing on its own, and the first customer. Dubbed the Arm AGI CPU, it's another...

item.title: Apple is testing a standalone app for its overhauled Siri
item.description: Apple's efforts to rebuild its Apple Intelligence AI platform will make its debut at its Worldwide Developers Conference on June 8th. A new version of Siri that transforms the voice assistant into...

item.title: Databricks bought two startups to underpin its new AI security product
item.description: With an overflowing war chest from its recent $5 billion raise, Databricks is buying startups and looking for more. It acquired Antimatter and SiftD.ai.

item.title: The man who coined Metaverse now says Meta’s glasses are creepy
item.description: Neal Stephenson didn't invent the virtual reality headset. But Meta certainly knows his name - in 1992, his seminal cyberpunk novel Snow Crash coined the phrase "Metaverse" to describe a virtual...

item.title: Arm is releasing the first in-house chip in its 35-year history
item.description: Arm is producing its own CPU for the first time. It developed the CPU with Meta, which is also the chip's first customer.

item.title: OpenAI’s plans to make ChatGPT more like Amazon aren’t going so well
item.description: OpenAI says it's moving away from Instant Checkout, which allowed users to buy items directly through the ChatGPT interface.

item.title: Apple launches iOS 26.4 with AI playlists, purchase sharing, and more
item.description: iOS 26.4 is here, and it comes with a bunch of small but notable updates. That includes a new Playlist Playground launching in beta in Apple Music, which uses AI to generate a song playlist -...

item.title: Google TV’s new Gemini features keep fans updated on sports teams and more
item.description: Three Gemini-powered features are coming to your Google TV. This includes visual responses, deep dives, and sports briefs.

item.title: OpenAI adds open source tools to help developers build for teen safety
item.description: Rather than working from scratch to figure out how to make AI safer for teens, developers can use these policies to fortify what they build.

item.title: Talat’s AI meeting notes stay on your machine, not in the cloud
item.description: The subscription-free AI meeting notes app is a local-first twist on notetaking tools like Granola.

item.title: The Lego Game Boy makes for a great gift, and it’s $10 off today
item.description: Deals on the Lego Game Boy are virtually nonexistent, save for the $10 discount that Costco and Sam’s Club offered their members when the set launched late last year. The deal is back — this time,...

item.title: TeamPCP Backdoors LiteLLM Versions 1.82.7–1.82.8 Likely via Trivy CI/CD Compromise
item.description: TeamPCP, the threat actor behind the recent compromises of Trivy and KICS, has now compromised a popular Python package named litellm, pushing two malicious versions containing a credential...

item.title: Exclusive eBook: Are we ready to hand AI agents the keys?
item.description: We’re starting to give AI agents real autonomy, but are we prepared for what could happen next? This subscriber-only eBook explores this and angles from experts, such as “If we continue on the...

item.title: Doss raises $55M for AI inventory management that plugs into ERP
item.description: Doss's AI-powered inventory management system integrates with existing ERP systems. The Series B round was co-led by Madrona and Premji Invest.

item.title: The Apple Watch SE 3 is even easier to recommend at $50 off
item.description: When Apple released the latest Apple Watch SE, it surprised us, outshining Apple’s more expensive models while improving on the basics. Now that it’s on sale, it’s even easier to recommend. The...

item.title: ChatGPT and Gemini are fighting to be the AI bot that sells you stuff
item.description: The AI-powered shopping rivalry is heating up as Google and OpenAI launch new features to help you buy things while interacting with their chatbots. Now, Google is teaming up with Gap Inc to allow...

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign active since January 2026 has been observed targeting U.S.-based individuals searching for tax-related documents to serve rogue installers for ConnectWise...

item.title: Arm Is Now Making Its Own Chips
item.description: The chip design firm says Meta, OpenAI, Cerebras, and Cloudflare are among the first customers of its new artificial intelligence hardware.

item.title: Welp, I bought an iPhone again
item.description: It takes a week to switch phones. First, there's the technical process of moving eSIMs across devices, which takes either a few minutes (if you're switching from one Android phone to another) or...

item.title: This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain
item.description: L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits cushioned in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature of around −146 degrees °C for over a decade, largely undisturbed. That...
